Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails using search_emails with query related to "Chloe" and "coordinator" or "resume" or "hire" (or similar) to find Chloe's forwarded email to Elena about the coordinator candidate. Expected findings: email from chloe.vance@moveops.com to elena.rostova@moveops.com forwarding Nina Sethi's application from SentinelChain Logistics in Denver. Chloe recommends scheduling an interview, notes Nina has 6 years experience, DOT hazmat certification, international experience, and FIDI FAIM certification. Chloe mentions the capacity problem and that the Terraform coordinator role has not been posted.
OE 2: Search emails using search_emails with query related to "Nina Sethi" or "SentinelChain" (or similar) to find Nina's original application email. Expected findings: email from nina.sethi@sentinelchain-logistics.com to chloe.vance@moveops.com with her background, qualifications, and that Fatimah Al-Rashidi referred her. Also discover a second email from Nina to suki.patel@moveops.com, a casual catch-up where Nina reveals that SentinelChain has been shortlisted for a contract with NorthWind Technologies to handle their server infrastructure relocations on an ongoing basis, with RFP requirements for supervised loading, real-time GPS tracking, and no-warehouse-storage.
OE 3: Search Slack using conversations_search_messages with search_query related to "SentinelChain" or "NorthWind" and "shortlisted" (or similar) to find David Chen's post in #executive about the competitive intelligence. Expected findings: David posted in #executive (C004) that SentinelChain has been shortlisted for a NorthWind chain-of-custody contract, and combined with PathFinder, NorthWind appears to be building a replacement vendor stack. David addresses Elena by name and says the retention proposal window is closing faster than expected.
OE 4: Search CRM using crm_search_companies with name "NorthWind" (or similar) and crm_search_deals with dealname "NorthWind" (or similar) to assess the NorthWind account status. Expected findings: NorthWind Technologies (company_northwind, type: Customer) is facing churn risk. Deals include deal_northwind_denver_expansion (Denver Satellite Office Expansion, 2 relocations) and deal_northwind_denver_expansion_v2. The churn threat is not reflected in a deal stage field but is documented in Pam Kowalski's final warning email and CRM engagement eng_northwind_sentinelchain_intel_apr23 which records the SentinelChain competitive intelligence from David Chen.
OE 5: Search CRM using crm_list_engagements with company_ids for NorthWind to find engagement notes. Expected findings: engagement note documenting SentinelChain shortlisted for NorthWind chain-of-custody contract, source Suki Patel via Nina Sethi, noting NorthWind is unbundling services with PathFinder plus SentinelChain as a replacement vendor stack.
OE 6: Search emails using search_emails with query related to "GreenStack" and "damage" or "battery" or "hazmat" or "incident" (or similar) to find the GreenStack lithium damage thread. Expected findings: (a) email from greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com to fatimah.al-rashidi@moveops.com describing the damage as a "minor crack on the outer casing" with "small amount of electrolyte on the warehouse floor" and stating "no action needed on your end," (b) email from derek.hollis@greenstackenergy.com to emeka.diallo@moveops.com demanding a formal EHS incident report with 5 specific items, module valued at approximately $87,500, and mentioning potential Oregon DEQ reporting obligation under ORS 466.635, (c) Emeka's reply promising to investigate but no follow-up delivered.
OE 7: Search Slack using conversations_search_messages with search_query related to "GreenStack" and "lithium" or "battery" or "cosmetic damage" (or similar) to find the #operations discussion. Expected findings: Fatimah posted in #operations (C006) relaying Greg's update, describing it as "cosmetic damage" and noting the crew cleaned it up and the module is functional. Chloe replied in the thread challenging the "cosmetic damage" characterization and demanding actual incident details, noting this is a Class 9 hazmat shipment during an active DOT audit.
OE 8: Search Linear using linear_list_issues with query related to "GreenStack" or "lithium" or "battery" or "Swift Portland" (or similar) to find the tracking ticket. Expected findings: issue_greenstack_lithium_spill in proj_greenstack / team_operations, titled "GreenStack battery module damage - Swift Portland warehouse incident," priority 2 (High), due April 28, describing the need to verify crack severity, electrolyte leakage, decontamination standards, and regulatory reporting obligations.
OE 9: Search emails using search_emails with query related to "Swift" and "suspension" or "formal notice" or "ultimatum" (or similar) to find the Swift service suspension threat. Expected findings: formal notice from greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com to marcus.thorne@moveops.com threatening to suspend all MoveOps shipments effective Tuesday, April 28, 2026 unless three conditions are met: (1) full payment of $11,010 outstanding balance, (2) execution of revised rate schedule with 18% surcharge and $400 hazmat premium, (3) written assumption of DOT documentation responsibility. Deadline: Monday, April 27 at 5:00 PM CDT. Also find Marcus's reply requesting a 5-business-day extension with no response from Greg.
OE 10: Search Slack using conversations_search_messages with search_query related to "Swift" and "escalating" or "working on it" (or similar) in #finance to find Marcus's cryptic message. Expected findings: Marcus posted in #finance (C005) on April 25: "Swift situation is escalating. I am working on it and will have more clarity by Monday. Flagging so the team is aware there may be vendor capacity changes next week." This is the vague message Elena referenced in the prompt, notable for omitting the suspension threat, the deadline, and the specific demands.
OE 11: Search QuickBooks using quickbooks_search_vendors with criteria for "Swift" (or similar) to verify the outstanding balance. Expected findings: vendor_swift (Swift Relocations) with Balance of $11,010, confirming the amount cited in the ultimatum email.
OE 12: Search Airtable using airtable_search_records with base_id "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name "Relocations", field_name "Company", value "GreenStack" (or similar) to find the Mei-Lin Zhao relocation record. Expected findings: recMeiLinZhaoHoustonPortland showing Mei-Lin Zhao, GreenStack Energy, Status: In Progress, Origin: Houston TX, Destination: Portland OR, Assigned Coordinator: Fatimah Al-Rashidi, Special Requirements detailing HAZMAT Class 9 lithium-ion battery test modules with UN3481 certification, climate-controlled transport, and split shipment requirements.
OE 13: Search emails or Slack for the existing DOT/PHMSA compliance audit context using search_emails with query related to "PHMSA" or "DOT compliance" or "hazmat audit" (or similar). Expected findings: the active DOT compliance inquiry (Case No. PHMSA-2026-04-1187), 15-day extension granted with deadline June 5, 2026, potential penalties up to $59,017/day. Elena posted a company-wide DOT compliance policy in #general on April 22.
OE 14: Look up contacts using contacts_search_contacts with query "Nina Sethi" (or similar) to verify her identity and employer. Expected findings: Nina Sethi, Dispatch Coordinator at SentinelChain Logistics, Denver CO, phone (303) 555-0182, email nina.sethi@sentinelchain-logistics.com. Her description mentions she handled Raj Patel's supervised server lab pickup for NorthWind, confirming SentinelChain already has a working relationship with NorthWind.
OE 15: Search emails or Slack for the existing NorthWind churn crisis context using search_emails with query related to "Pam Kowalski" or "NorthWind" and "final warning" or "PathFinder" (or similar). Expected findings: Pam Kowalski's formal escalation and final warning email to david.chen@moveops.com (CC victor.huang@northwindtech.com) citing chain-of-custody breach on Raj Patel's server lab, piano damage on Emilia Cruz's move, and the missed service recovery follow-through Catalina committed to on April 14. Pam states NorthWind has started preliminary conversations with PathFinder Logistics and needs a concrete retention proposal by end of day Friday.
OE 16: Send an email (via send_email or reply_to_email on a relevant thread) from elena.rostova@moveops.com to marcus.thorne@moveops.com, CC chloe.vance@moveops.com and david.chen@moveops.com, with the risk briefing summary covering all three interlinked situations: the Swift suspension threat and its deadline, the GreenStack lithium damage incident and its regulatory implications, and the SentinelChain/NorthWind competitive intelligence and its connection to the coordinator hiring decision.
OE 17: Send an email (via send_email or reply_to_email on a relevant thread) from elena.rostova@moveops.com to chloe.vance@moveops.com regarding the coordinator hiring decision, informing Chloe that the Nina Sethi hire is on hold pending resolution of the SentinelChain/NorthWind competitive conflict, and directing Chloe to escalate the GreenStack hazmat incident formally given the DOT audit.
OE 18: Post a message (via conversations_add_message) in #executive (channel_id: C004) with payload summarizing the three interlinked risks (Swift suspension, GreenStack hazmat incident, SentinelChain/NorthWind competitive threat) and flagging decisions needed before Monday.
OE 19: Create a Linear issue (via linear_create_issue) for the Swift vendor suspension crisis, with team "Operations" or "Engineering" (or similar), title related to "Swift service suspension risk" or "Vendor continuity crisis" (or similar), description covering the 72-hour ultimatum, $11,010 outstanding balance, rate increase demands, and Monday deadline.
OE 20: Create a CRM engagement (via crm_create_engagement) with engagement_type "NOTE" on the NorthWind account (company_ids including company_northwind), supplementing the existing eng_northwind_sentinelchain_intel_apr23 with the combined competitive threat assessment: SentinelChain shortlisted for chain-of-custody contract, PathFinder pitching general relocation, the connection to coordinator candidate Nina Sethi, and the implications of the Swift vendor suspension for NorthWind service continuity.
OE 21: Update the existing Linear issue for the GreenStack lithium spill (via linear_update_issue on issue_greenstack_lithium_spill or similar) to escalate priority given the connection to the Swift ultimatum and DOT audit, or create a comment (via linear_create_comment) adding the cross-scenario context.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

1 An email was sent (via send_email, reply_to_email, or forward_email) from [elena.rostova@moveops.com](mailto:elena.rostova@moveops.com) with [marcus.thorne@moveops.com](mailto:marcus.thorne@moveops.com), [chloe.vance@moveops.com](mailto:chloe.vance@moveops.com), and [david.chen@moveops.com](mailto:david.chen@moveops.com) included as recipients or CC (in any combination).
outcome
 ✓✓✗✗✓✓ 67%
✓Run #1The send_email tool call was made with sender elena.rostova@moveops.com and recipients including david.chen@moveops.com, chloe.vance@moveops.com, and marcus.thorne@moveops.com (plus emeka.diallo@moveops.com). All three required department heads are included as direct recipients.
✓Run #2The send_email tool was called with sender elena.rostova@moveops.com and recipients david.chen@moveops.com, marcus.thorne@moveops.com, chloe.vance@moveops.com, and emeka.diallo@moveops.com. All three required recipients (marcus, chloe, david) are present.
✗Run #3No send_email, reply_to_email, or forward_email tool call was found in the trajectory. The agent posted Slack messages instead but did not send any email to marcus.thorne@moveops.com, chloe.vance@moveops.com, or david.chen@moveops.com.

✓Run #1Both the sent email and final response explicitly reference Greg Pallone at Swift characterizing the damage as 'cosmetic' or 'minor.' The email states 'Greg Pallone at Swift saying one of the GreenStack lithium battery modules had cosmetic damage' and the final response says Swift's characterization was 'Minor crack on outer casing, small amount of electrolyte on warehouse floor. Crew cleaned it up. Module fully functional.'
✓Run #2The email states Greg Pallone described the damage as 'cosmetic damage' and said his crew 'cleaned it up,' which is the carrier's downplaying characterization. The final response also notes Greg called it 'cosmetic.' This matches the criterion's requirement to report Greg's/Swift's characterization of the damage as minor/cosmetic/requiring no action.
✓Run #3The final response states: 'Carrier says: "Cosmetic damage. Cleaned it up. No action needed."' — this directly captures Swift/Greg's characterization of the damage as minor/cosmetic requiring no action.
✓Run #4The final response states 'Swift says: cosmetic damage, cleaned up, functional, no action needed.' The Slack post further details Greg's description as 'Cosmetic damage,' 'minor crack,' crew cleaned it up, module 'fully functional.' Greg's downplaying characterization is clearly reported.
✓Run #5The email explicitly states: 'Swift says: Greg Pallone told Fatimah it was cosmetic damage during unloading and his crew cleaned it up.' The risk table also notes 'Cosmetic vs. electrolyte residue is the difference between minor and potentially reportable.'
✓Run #6The final response states 'Greg Pallone told Fatimah one battery module had "cosmetic damage" during unloading at their Portland warehouse. Crew "cleaned it up." Module "functional."' This accurately represents the carrier's (Greg/Swift) downplaying characterization.
21 The final response or risk briefing identifies that Derek Hollis (GreenStack) or GreenStack's EHS team characterized the same damage as a serious hazmat incident requiring formal documentation.
outcome
 ✓✗✓✓✓✓ 83%
✓Run #1Both the sent email and the final response explicitly attribute to GreenStack's EHS team (Dr. Yuki Tanabe) the characterization of the damage as a potential hazmat incident requiring formal documentation. The contrast with the carrier's version is explicitly drawn in both.
✗Run #2The agent never found Derek Hollis's email or GreenStack's EHS team characterization. The email only mentions that Chloe flagged it as potentially serious — not the client's own characterization of it as a serious hazmat incident requiring formal documentation.
✓Run #3The final response clearly states 'GreenStack's EHS Director Dr. Yuki Tanabe flagged it as a potential hazmat incident. She's requiring a formal incident report' — directly presenting the client's serious characterization contrasting the carrier's view.
✓Run #4The final response states 'GreenStack says: Their EHS director is requiring a formal incident report. Oregon DEQ reporting may be mandatory...' The Slack post further identifies Dr. Yuki Tanabe requiring the incident report and characterizes the damage as a 'cracked outer casing with visible electrolyte residue.' The client's serious characterization is clearly reported.
✓Run #5The email clearly describes Derek Hollis and GreenStack's EHS director Dr. Yuki Tanabe flagging the damage as 'a potential hazmat incident' requiring a formal incident report and possibly mandatory Oregon DEQ regulatory reporting. The client's version is explicitly contrasted with Swift's 'cosmetic' characterization.
✓Run #6The final response and email both state that 'Derek Hollis escalated formally on April 24. Mei-Lin reported a cracked outer casing with visible electrolyte residue. GreenStack's EHS director Dr. Yuki Tanabe is evaluating mandatory Oregon DEQ reporting.' The client's serious characterization is clearly presented.
